repair_batches.description: char_gicho complete 15-frame rebuild from locked model sheet
char_ritsu + char_umeko all expressions from locked refs
Remaining expression text removal + react_jump improvement
3 tile text regeneration + 5 tile saturation correction

iteration_notes: Iteration 1 Codex review COMPLETED — 85 assets inspected, 6 blockers and 7 majors found.
Feedback report written to image-feedback-report.json covering all blocker/major findings.
Repair has NOT been executed yet — repair batches defined and prioritized.
Iteration 1 passes audit structure checks: Codex produced evidence-grounded JSON + MD, all batches inspected.
Positive findings to preserve: tile distinguishability OK, special tile markers CORRECT, wave motion visible, most portraits crop-correct, Hinata/Kenzo/Natsuka/Fuyuko expressions largely consistent.
Iteration 2 should begin with REPAIR-A (gicho rebuild) as highest-risk item, then REPAIR-B+C+D in parallel if generation capacity allows.

repair_actions.rationale: Gicho frame animation identity drift is a fundamental text-to-image limitation. Each frame is an independent generation with no reference. Accepted as design-phase placeholder art. Known limitation — professional frame-by-frame animation required for production.

repair_actions.change_from_repair1: Added ABSOLUTE FIXED APPEARANCE anchor with explicit age/face-shape/bun-shape/apron consistency instruction across all 5 emotions
New repair — portrait was not in repair batch 1. Locked: dark disheveled hair, blue-grey rolled-sleeve shirt, same face as default portrait.
repair_actions.rerun_notes: Broad hair/shirt markers now match but face structure and rendering style differ from portrait_default. Text-to-image limitation.

results.notes: Umeko: bun and bamboo-green apron persist, but age, face shape, and body scale still drift across the 5 emotions despite ABSOLUTE FIXED APPEARANCE anchor in repair2 prompts. Ritsu expressions pass — consistent identity confirmed.

Ritsu portrait_happy: dark disheveled hair and blue-grey shirt now present (correct broad cues), but face structure and chibi rendering style still differ from portrait_default. Hinata portrait_happy confirmed PASS.

iterations.root_cause: Fundamental text-to-image limitation: no reference image input means each generation independently interprets the character description. Subtle features (age lines, face geometry, scale) cannot be locked through prompt text alone.
options: A: Accept both as design-phase placeholders (same decision as Gicho animation)
B: Attempt repair3 with stronger anchoring or explicit dimension constraints
C: Use img2img with portrait_default as reference image for Ritsu; use default expression as reference for Umeko

overall_notes: Two blockers remain after 2 repair iterations. Root cause is text-to-image identity drift — a fundamental limitation without img2img. Decision required: accept as placeholder (Option A), attempt repair3 (Option B, last budget for BATCH-03), or img2img approach (Option C).
